articles.headline: India Launches 'Amrit Bharat Green Energy Grid' Initiative to Accelerate Energy Transition
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Power officially launched the 'Amrit Bharat Green Energy Grid' (ABGEG) initiative, a flagship program aimed at integrating renewable energy sources across the national grid.
• The initiative proposes significant investments in inter-state transmission systems, smart grid technologies, and energy storage solutions to handle the increasing share of intermittent renewable energy.
• ABGEG targets achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel electricity capacity by 2030, a key commitment under India's Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
• The project will involve public-private partnerships and leverage advanced grid management systems, including AI and IoT, for optimal energy flow and stability.
• It is expected to create millions of green jobs and position India as a global leader in renewable energy integration and grid modernization.

Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life

mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of ABGEG in achieving India's climate goals and energy security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in integrating a high share of renewable energy into the national grid.
Evaluate the role of technology (AI, IoT, ESS) and financing models (PPP) in successful implementation.
Examine the socio-economic impact, including job creation and regional development.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized map showing interconnected green energy hubs across India with transmission lines.
articles.trivia: India's installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) crossed 100 GW in 2021, a significant step towards its ambitious targets.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Verdict on Right to Digital Privacy in the Age of AI
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a significant judgment, reinforcing the fundamental Right to Digital Privacy, extending its scope to cover data generated and processed by Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems.
• The ruling mandates greater transparency from private and public entities regarding the collection, storage, and algorithmic processing of personal data, especially in AI-driven decision-making.
• It calls for a robust data protection framework that includes provisions for data localization, consent mechanisms, and the 'right to be forgotten' in the digital realm.
• The judgment emphasizes the need for a comprehensive legal framework to regulate AI and its potential impact on individual liberties and democratic processes.
• This verdict is expected to have far-reaching implications for data governance, technology companies, and government surveillance policies.

articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block with binary code overlaid, symbolizing law and digital data.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Management Amidst Global Volatility
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, opting to maintain the benchmark repo rate at 6.50% for the seventh consecutive time.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted persistent global inflationary pressures and geopolitical uncertainties as key factors influencing the decision, alongside robust domestic growth.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target while supporting growth, maintaining a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Projections for FY 2025-26 indicate a GDP growth of 6.8% and CPI inflation at 4.7%, contingent on monsoon performance and global crude oil prices.
• Measures were also announced to enhance liquidity management and strengthen the financial sector's resilience.

articles.headline: India and France Deepen Strategic Partnership with New Defense & Space Accords
articles.explainer:
• India and France signed a series of agreements aimed at significantly enhancing their strategic partnership, particularly in the defense and space sectors.
• The accords include provisions for joint development of advanced military technologies, expanded maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific, and reciprocal logistics support.
• A key highlight is the 'India-France Space Technology Exchange Program', focusing on satellite navigation, earth observation, and climate monitoring applications.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing multilateralism and rules-based international order.
• These agreements underscore the growing convergence of interests between Delhi and Paris in regional security and technological collaboration.

articles.headline: NTCA Releases Mid-Term Review of Tiger Reserves, Highlights Conservation Successes and Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) released its mid-term review report on the status of tiger reserves across India, providing an update on conservation efforts since the last major census.
• The report indicates a stable to increasing tiger population in several key reserves, attributing success to enhanced anti-poaching measures, habitat improvement, and community involvement.
• However, it also highlighted persistent challenges, including human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation due to infrastructure projects, and the impact of climate change on tiger habitats.
• The NTCA recommended strengthening corridor connectivity, promoting sustainable tourism models, and integrating local communities more effectively into conservation strategies.
• The findings will guide future conservation policies and resource allocation for Project Tiger.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a major milestone with the successful test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a space vehicle from orbit, demonstrating critical technologies for future two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch systems.
• This achievement is crucial for significantly reducing the cost of space launches, making space access more affordable and sustainable for India.
• The RLV-TD program aims to develop fully autonomous winged body return capability, crucial for future manned missions and satellite deployment.
• Further tests are planned to validate aerodynamic characteristics, thermal protection systems, and autonomous navigation for orbital re-entry.

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana 2.0' with Expanded Coverage and Benefits
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched 'Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) 2.0', an enhanced version of the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.
• PMJAY 2.0 expands the coverage to include more vulnerable sections of the population and increases the insurance cover per family to ₹7 lakh from the previous ₹5 lakh.
• New benefits include coverage for a wider range of critical illnesses, preventive healthcare packages, and mental health services, emphasizing holistic well-being.
• The scheme aims to strengthen the digital health infrastructure, integrating PMJAY 2.0 with the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission for seamless service delivery.
• This initiative is a major step towards achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC) and reducing out-of-pocket expenditure for healthcare in India.

articles.headline: Border Security Force Enhances Surveillance along Western Borders with AI Integration
articles.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) has initiated a major technological upgrade, integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) powered surveillance systems along critical stretches of India's western borders.
• These advanced systems, comprising drones, ground sensors, and high-resolution cameras, can detect, identify, and track suspicious activities in real-time, significantly improving response capabilities.
• The AI integration aims to reduce human error, enhance situational awareness, and provide predictive intelligence for preventing infiltration, smuggling, and cross-border terrorism.
• This move is part of the Comprehensive Integrated Border Management System (CIBMS) and underscores India's commitment to modernizing its border security apparatus.
• Training programs for BSF personnel on operating and maintaining these sophisticated AI tools are also being scaled up.

articles.headline: Ministry of Railways Announces Significant Progress on Dedicated Freight Corridors (DFCs)
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Railways provided an update on the progress of India's Dedicated Freight Corridors (DFCs), highlighting the operationalization of over 90% of the Eastern and Western DFCs.
• The DFCs are crucial for decongesting existing railway lines, enabling faster movement of goods, and reducing logistics costs, thereby boosting India's economic competitiveness.
• The operational segments have already demonstrated significant improvements in freight train speeds and turnaround times, attracting more cargo from road transport.
• Future plans include extending the DFC network to new industrial hubs and integrating advanced signaling and control systems for enhanced efficiency and safety.
• This infrastructure push is central to the National Logistics Policy and 'Make in India' initiative, supporting industrial growth and export competitiveness.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement Review Concludes with Enhanced Provisions
articles.explainer:
• India and the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) successfully concluded the review of their Free Trade Agreement (FTA) in Goods, agreeing on enhanced provisions to boost bilateral trade.
• The revised FTA (AITIGA) aims to address existing trade imbalances, simplify rules of origin, and reduce non-tariff barriers, facilitating smoother trade flows.
• Key areas of improvement include greater market access for Indian goods, particularly in textiles, pharmaceuticals, and agricultural products, and streamlined customs procedures.
• Both sides expressed optimism that the modernized agreement would significantly increase bilateral trade volumes, fostering economic integration and supply chain resilience.
• This updated agreement is crucial for India's 'Act East Policy' and its engagement with the dynamic Southeast Asian region.

articles.headline: New National Water Policy 2025 Emphasizes Integrated River Basin Management
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the new 'National Water Policy 2025', replacing the previous policy, with a strong emphasis on integrated river basin management and water resource sustainability.
• The policy prioritizes water security for all, promoting efficient water use in agriculture, industry, and domestic sectors through demand-side management and water-saving technologies.
• It advocates for a paradigm shift from a supply-side approach to a holistic, basin-level planning and management, involving all riparian states and local communities.
• Key provisions include promoting water recycling and reuse, groundwater recharge, and the use of advanced data analytics for water resource assessment and forecasting.
• The policy also addresses challenges posed by climate change on water availability and quality, proposing adaptive strategies for water management.

Water resource management

articles.headline: Election Commission of India Proposes Reforms for Transparent Political Party Funding
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) submitted a comprehensive proposal to the government, recommending significant reforms to enhance transparency in political party funding.
• The proposals include lowering the anonymous donation limit from ₹20,000 to ₹2,000 and mandating disclosure of all donations, regardless of amount, through a digital platform.
• ECI also suggested making electoral bonds more transparent by requiring the issuing bank to disclose the purchaser's identity to the ECI.
• The reforms aim to curb the use of black money in elections, promote ethical political financing, and strengthen democratic accountability.
• These recommendations follow long-standing concerns about the opacity of political funding and its potential influence on electoral outcomes.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Gene Editing Technology for Rare Genetic Disorders
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists at the National Centre for Biological Sciences (NCBS) announced a significant breakthrough in CRISPR-based gene editing technology, offering new hope for treating rare genetic disorders.
• The research focused on developing a more precise and efficient gene-editing tool that can target specific mutations responsible for conditions like Sickle Cell Anemia and Thalassemia, with reduced off-target effects.
• This indigenous advancement in biotechnology holds immense potential for personalized medicine and reducing reliance on expensive imported gene therapies.
• Clinical trials for the new gene-editing approach are anticipated to begin within the next two years, following rigorous safety and efficacy assessments.
• The breakthrough underscores India's growing capabilities in advanced biomedical research and its commitment to addressing public health challenges.

articles.headline: UNESCO Designates New Indian Heritage Sites, Boosts Cultural Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) announced the inclusion of two new sites from India on its World Heritage List: the 'Mughal Gardens of Delhi' and the 'Ancient Rock-Cut Caves of Unakoti' in Tripura.
• The designation recognizes the outstanding universal value of these sites, highlighting India's rich historical, architectural, and cultural heritage.
• This inclusion is expected to significantly boost cultural tourism to these regions, attracting international visitors and generating local employment.
• It also places a greater responsibility on India for the preservation and sustainable management of these newly recognized heritage sites.
• The Ministry of Culture and Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) will collaborate with UNESCO on conservation efforts and promoting these sites globally.

articles.headline: India's Exports Surge in Q4 FY25, Driven by Manufacturing and Services Sector Growth
articles.explainer:
• India's merchandise and services exports witnessed a significant surge in the fourth quarter of Fiscal Year 2024-25, indicating a strong performance amidst global economic headwinds.
• The growth was primarily propelled by robust demand for Indian manufactured goods, particularly in engineering, electronics, and textiles, as well as a strong rebound in IT and business services exports.
• Government initiatives like Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes and efforts to diversify export markets are cited as key contributors to this positive trend.
• This export growth is crucial for narrowing the trade deficit, strengthening the rupee, and supporting overall economic stability and job creation.
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry expressed confidence in achieving ambitious export targets for the upcoming fiscal year, leveraging new trade agreements.

articles.headline: Major Cities to Implement 'Clean Air Action Plan 2.0' to Combat Air Pollution
articles.explainer:
• Several metropolitan cities and urban agglomerations across India announced the phased implementation of the 'Clean Air Action Plan 2.0', an upgraded strategy to tackle severe air pollution.
• The plan focuses on source-specific interventions, including stringent emission norms for industries, promotion of electric vehicles, and improved waste management practices.
• It integrates real-time air quality monitoring systems and leverages AI-driven predictive models to issue pollution alerts and guide targeted actions.
• The new plan emphasizes public awareness campaigns and citizen participation, encouraging behavioral changes for reducing individual carbon footprints.
• This initiative is part of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) and aims to achieve a 40% reduction in particulate matter concentration by 2030.

articles.headline: Panchayati Raj Institutions to Get Enhanced Digital Infrastructure Support for e-Governance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Panchayati Raj announced a major initiative to significantly enhance digital infrastructure and e-governance capabilities across Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) nationwide.
• The program aims to equip all Gram Panchayats with high-speed internet connectivity, digital devices, and specialized software for efficient service delivery and administrative functions.
• This move is expected to empower local self-governments, improve transparency, and streamline the implementation of various rural development schemes.
• Training modules for Panchayat functionaries on digital literacy and e-governance tools will be rolled out to ensure effective utilization of the new infrastructure.
• The initiative aligns with the 'Digital India' vision, bridging the rural-urban digital divide and fostering grassroots democracy.

• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to accelerating reforms, emphasizing stakeholder collaboration and adaptive strategies to overcome implementation hurdles.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century and foster holistic development.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meeting Concludes with Focus on Global Debt and Climate Finance
articles.explainer:
• The G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting concluded in Washington D.C., with member nations reaffirming commitment to global economic stability.
• Key discussions revolved around addressing rising sovereign debt vulnerabilities in developing countries and enhancing multilateral coordination for sustainable debt restructuring.
• India emphasized the need for increased and accessible climate finance for developing nations, pushing for concrete action on the $100 billion annual climate finance goal.
• The meeting also explored strategies for strengthening the global financial safety net and reforming multilateral development banks to better respond to contemporary challenges.
• Participants agreed on the importance of combating inflation while safeguarding economic growth, navigating complex geopolitical and economic uncertainties.

articles.headline: Archaeologists Uncover New Insights into Indus Valley Civilization's Decline and Climate Change Resilience
articles.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations at a site in Haryana have yielded new evidence, offering insights into the complex factors contributing to the decline of the mature Indus Valley Civilization (IVC).
• The findings suggest a gradual shift in settlement patterns and subsistence strategies, indicating a prolonged period of adaptation to changing climatic conditions, particularly weakening monsoons.
• Researchers uncovered advanced water harvesting systems and crop diversification, showcasing the IVC's remarkable resilience before its eventual de-urbanization.
• The study challenges earlier theories of a sudden collapse, proposing a more nuanced understanding of how ancient societies responded to environmental pressures.
• This new data is crucial for understanding the interplay between climate, environment, and human societies in ancient India.

articles.headline: Indian Pharmaceutical Sector Poised for Robust Growth with Focus on R&D and Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Indian pharmaceutical sector is projected for robust growth in the coming decade, driven by increased R&D investments, expansion into new therapeutic areas, and a strong export strategy.
• Industry reports highlight India's potential to become a global hub for pharmaceutical innovation, moving beyond its traditional role as a producer of generic drugs.
• Government support through favorable policies, Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes for bulk drugs and medical devices, and streamlined regulatory processes are catalyzing this growth.
• Focus areas include biopharmaceuticals, precision medicine, and advanced vaccine development, catering to both domestic and international markets.
• This expansion is expected to enhance India's healthcare security, create high-skilled jobs, and contribute significantly to the national GDP.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Amrit Bharat Green Energy Grid' (ABGEG) initiative, consider the following statements:
1. It is launched by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
2. It aims to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel electricity capacity by 2030.
3. It primarily focuses on offshore wind energy projects.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The 'Amrit Bharat Green Energy Grid' (ABGEG) initiative is launched by the Union Ministry of Power. Statement 2 is correct. The initiative targets achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel electricity capacity by 2030. Statement 3 is incorrect. While offshore wind may be part of renewable energy, the initiative broadly focuses on integrating all renewable energy sources across the national grid, including inter-state transmission, smart grid technologies, and energy storage solutions.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for submitting proposals for reforms to enhance transparency in political party funding in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is the autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes and proposing reforms related to electoral matters, including political party funding, to the government.

• This inclusion is expected to significantly boost cultural tourism to these regions, attracting international visitors and generating local employment.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Verdict on Right to Digital Privacy in the Age of AI
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a significant judgment, reinforcing the fundamental Right to Digital Privacy, extending its scope to cover data generated and processed by Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems.
• The ruling mandates greater transparency from private and public entities regarding the collection, storage, and algorithmic processing of personal data, especially in AI-driven decision-making.
• It calls for a robust data protection framework that includes provisions for data localization, consent mechanisms, and the 'right to be forgotten' in the digital realm.
• The judgment emphasizes the need for a comprehensive legal framework to regulate AI and its potential impact on individual liberties and democratic processes.
• This verdict is expected to have far-reaching implications for data governance, technology companies, and government surveillance policies.

• PMJAY 2.0 expands the coverage to include more vulnerable sections of the population and increases the insurance cover per family to ₹7 lakh from the previous ₹5 lakh.
• New benefits include coverage for a wider range of critical illnesses, preventive healthcare packages, and mental health services, emphasizing holistic well-being.
• The scheme aims to strengthen the digital health infrastructure, integrating PMJAY 2.0 with the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission for seamless service delivery.
• This initiative is a major step towards achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC) and reducing out-of-pocket expenditure for healthcare in India.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Free Trade Agreement Review Concludes with Enhanced Provisions
GS2.explainer:

• The revised FTA (AITIGA) aims to address existing trade imbalances, simplify rules of origin, and reduce non-tariff barriers, facilitating smoother trade flows.
• Key areas of improvement include greater market access for Indian goods, particularly in textiles, pharmaceuticals, and agricultural products, and streamlined customs procedures.
• Both sides expressed optimism that the modernized agreement would significantly increase bilateral trade volumes, fostering economic integration and supply chain resilience.
• This updated agreement is crucial for India's 'Act East Policy' and its engagement with the dynamic Southeast Asian region.

GS2.headline: Election Commission of India Proposes Reforms for Transparent Political Party Funding
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) submitted a comprehensive proposal to the government, recommending significant reforms to enhance transparency in political party funding.
• The proposals include lowering the anonymous donation limit from ₹20,000 to ₹2,000 and mandating disclosure of all donations, regardless of amount, through a digital platform.
• ECI also suggested making electoral bonds more transparent by requiring the issuing bank to disclose the purchaser's identity to the ECI.
• The reforms aim to curb the use of black money in elections, promote ethical political financing, and strengthen democratic accountability.
• These recommendations follow long-standing concerns about the opacity of political funding and its potential influence on electoral outcomes.

• The Union Ministry of Power officially launched the 'Amrit Bharat Green Energy Grid' (ABGEG) initiative, a flagship program aimed at integrating renewable energy sources across the national grid.
• The initiative proposes significant investments in inter-state transmission systems, smart grid technologies, and energy storage solutions to handle the increasing share of intermittent renewable energy.
• ABGEG targets achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel electricity capacity by 2030, a key commitment under India's Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
• The project will involve public-private partnerships and leverage advanced grid management systems, including AI and IoT, for optimal energy flow and stability.
• It is expected to create millions of green jobs and position India as a global leader in renewable energy integration and grid modernization.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Management Amidst Global Volatility
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, opting to maintain the benchmark repo rate at 6.50% for the seventh consecutive time.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted persistent global inflationary pressures and geopolitical uncertainties as key factors influencing the decision, alongside robust domestic growth.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target while supporting growth, maintaining a 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Projections for FY 2025-26 indicate a GDP growth of 6.8% and CPI inflation at 4.7%, contingent on monsoon performance and global crude oil prices.
• Measures were also announced to enhance liquidity management and strengthen the financial sector's resilience.

GS3.headline: NTCA Releases Mid-Term Review of Tiger Reserves, Highlights Conservation Successes and Challenges
GS3.explainer:
• The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) released its mid-term review report on the status of tiger reserves across India, providing an update on conservation efforts since the last major census.
• The report indicates a stable to increasing tiger population in several key reserves, attributing success to enhanced anti-poaching measures, habitat improvement, and community involvement.
• However, it also highlighted persistent challenges, including human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation due to infrastructure projects, and the impact of climate change on tiger habitats.
• The NTCA recommended strengthening corridor connectivity, promoting sustainable tourism models, and integrating local communities more effectively into conservation strategies.
• The findings will guide future conservation policies and resource allocation for Project Tiger.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a major milestone with the successful test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a space vehicle from orbit, demonstrating critical technologies for future two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch systems.
• This achievement is crucial for significantly reducing the cost of space launches, making space access more affordable and sustainable for India.
• The RLV-TD program aims to develop fully autonomous winged body return capability, crucial for future manned missions and satellite deployment.
• Further tests are planned to validate aerodynamic characteristics, thermal protection systems, and autonomous navigation for orbital re-entry.

GS3.headline: Border Security Force Enhances Surveillance along Western Borders with AI Integration
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) has initiated a major technological upgrade, integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) powered surveillance systems along critical stretches of India's western borders.
• These advanced systems, comprising drones, ground sensors, and high-resolution cameras, can detect, identify, and track suspicious activities in real-time, significantly improving response capabilities.
• The AI integration aims to reduce human error, enhance situational awareness, and provide predictive intelligence for preventing infiltration, smuggling, and cross-border terrorism.
• This move is part of the Comprehensive Integrated Border Management System (CIBMS) and underscores India's commitment to modernizing its border security apparatus.
• Training programs for BSF personnel on operating and maintaining these sophisticated AI tools are also being scaled up.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Railways Announces Significant Progress on Dedicated Freight Corridors (DFCs)
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Railways provided an update on the progress of India's Dedicated Freight Corridors (DFCs), highlighting the operationalization of over 90% of the Eastern and Western DFCs.
• The DFCs are crucial for decongesting existing railway lines, enabling faster movement of goods, and reducing logistics costs, thereby boosting India's economic competitiveness.
• The operational segments have already demonstrated significant improvements in freight train speeds and turnaround times, attracting more cargo from road transport.
• Future plans include extending the DFC network to new industrial hubs and integrating advanced signaling and control systems for enhanced efficiency and safety.
• This infrastructure push is central to the National Logistics Policy and 'Make in India' initiative, supporting industrial growth and export competitiveness.

GS3.headline: New National Water Policy 2025 Emphasizes Integrated River Basin Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the new 'National Water Policy 2025', replacing the previous policy, with a strong emphasis on integrated river basin management and water resource sustainability.
• The policy prioritizes water security for all, promoting efficient water use in agriculture, industry, and domestic sectors through demand-side management and water-saving technologies.
• It advocates for a paradigm shift from a supply-side approach to a holistic, basin-level planning and management, involving all riparian states and local communities.
• Key provisions include promoting water recycling and reuse, groundwater recharge, and the use of advanced data analytics for water resource assessment and forecasting.
• The policy also addresses challenges posed by climate change on water availability and quality, proposing adaptive strategies for water management.

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Gene Editing Technology for Rare Genetic Disorders
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists at the National Centre for Biological Sciences (NCBS) announced a significant breakthrough in CRISPR-based gene editing technology, offering new hope for treating rare genetic disorders.
• The research focused on developing a more precise and efficient gene-editing tool that can target specific mutations responsible for conditions like Sickle Cell Anemia and Thalassemia, with reduced off-target effects.
• This indigenous advancement in biotechnology holds immense potential for personalized medicine and reducing reliance on expensive imported gene therapies.
• Clinical trials for the new gene-editing approach are anticipated to begin within the next two years, following rigorous safety and efficacy assessments.
• The breakthrough underscores India's growing capabilities in advanced biomedical research and its commitment to addressing public health challenges.

GS3.headline: India's Exports Surge in Q4 FY25, Driven by Manufacturing and Services Sector Growth
GS3.explainer:
• India's merchandise and services exports witnessed a significant surge in the fourth quarter of Fiscal Year 2024-25, indicating a strong performance amidst global economic headwinds.
• The growth was primarily propelled by robust demand for Indian manufactured goods, particularly in engineering, electronics, and textiles, as well as a strong rebound in IT and business services exports.
• Government initiatives like Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes and efforts to diversify export markets are cited as key contributors to this positive trend.
• This export growth is crucial for narrowing the trade deficit, strengthening the rupee, and supporting overall economic stability and job creation.
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry expressed confidence in achieving ambitious export targets for the upcoming fiscal year, leveraging new trade agreements.

GS3.headline: Major Cities to Implement 'Clean Air Action Plan 2.0' to Combat Air Pollution
GS3.explainer:
• Several metropolitan cities and urban agglomerations across India announced the phased implementation of the 'Clean Air Action Plan 2.0', an upgraded strategy to tackle severe air pollution.
• The plan focuses on source-specific interventions, including stringent emission norms for industries, promotion of electric vehicles, and improved waste management practices.
• It integrates real-time air quality monitoring systems and leverages AI-driven predictive models to issue pollution alerts and guide targeted actions.
• The new plan emphasizes public awareness campaigns and citizen participation, encouraging behavioral changes for reducing individual carbon footprints.
• This initiative is part of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) and aims to achieve a 40% reduction in particulate matter concentration by 2030.

GS3.headline: Indian Pharmaceutical Sector Poised for Robust Growth with Focus on R&D and Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian pharmaceutical sector is projected for robust growth in the coming decade, driven by increased R&D investments, expansion into new therapeutic areas, and a strong export strategy.
• Industry reports highlight India's potential to become a global hub for pharmaceutical innovation, moving beyond its traditional role as a producer of generic drugs.
• Government support through favorable policies, Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes for bulk drugs and medical devices, and streamlined regulatory processes are catalyzing this growth.
• Focus areas include biopharmaceuticals, precision medicine, and advanced vaccine development, catering to both domestic and international markets.
• This expansion is expected to enhance India's healthcare security, create high-skilled jobs, and contribute significantly to the national GDP.
